channel.title: Folklore on Alien Truth Social
channel.description: Recent content in Folklore on Alien Truth Social

item.title: St. Louis Light
item.description:
The St. Louis Light, also known as the St. Louis Ghost Light or Ghost Train, is a paranormal phenomenon reported near St. Louis, Saskatchewan, Canada. Witnesses describe it as a large white beam of light, similar to a locomotive’s headlamp, that moves along an abandoned rail line. This phenomenon has been featured on the television series Unsolved Mysteries and has inspired various folklore explanations, including a ghost train or the spirit of a brakeman. In 2014, Canada Post issued a stamp depicting the St. Louis ghost train as part of a series on Canadian ghost tales.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St._Louis_light

item.title: Chupacabra
item.description:
The chupacabra is a legendary creature in the folklore of the Americas, named for its alleged habit of attacking and drinking the blood of livestock. Descriptions of the creature vary, with some depicting it as reptilian and alien-like, while others describe it as more dog-like. Initial sightings occurred in Puerto Rico in 1995, and the creature has since been reported in various locations globally, though these reports are anecdotal and often attributed to animals like canids afflicted with mange. Investigations suggest that many sightings can be explained by known animals with diseases or by misidentification, with some descriptions potentially influenced by popular culture.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chupacabra

item.title: They Knew Too Much About Flying Saucers
item.description:
They Knew Too Much About Flying Saucers is a 1956 book by Gray Barker that holds historical significance as the first text to introduce the “Men in Black” trope into UFO conspiracy culture. Barker, a member of the International Flying Saucer Bureau, alleged that researchers like Albert K. Bender were being intimidated and silenced by mysterious figures to prevent the disclosure of extraterrestrial truths. While the book became a bestseller and influenced decades of paranormal folklore, later investigations—including comments by collaborator John Sherwood—suggest that Barker significantly embellished or fabricated these accounts to capitalize on public interest. The work ultimately shaped the foundational imagery for modern pop culture franchises, including The X-Files and the Men in Black film series.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/They_Knew_Too_Much_About_Flying_Saucers

item.title: Mutilation of "Snippy" the horse
item.description:
The 1967 death of an Appaloosa mare named “Snippy” near Alamosa, Colorado, gained national attention after reports suggested the animal had been mysteriously mutilated by extraterrestrial entities. While sensationalized media accounts linked the incident to UFOs, later investigations by the Condon Committee and others attributed the death to natural causes and gunshot wounds inflicted by local individuals. Despite these findings, the case remains a foundational element of UFO folklore and the study of alleged animal mutilations. The horse’s skeletal remains were ultimately acquired by the UFO Watchtower in 2021 as a tourist attraction.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mutilation_of_%22Snippy%22_the_horse

item.title: Archives for the Unexplained
item.description:
Archives for the Unexplained (AFU), formerly Archives for UFO research, is a nonprofit foundation in Norrköping, Sweden, dedicated to collecting materials on UFOs, ufology, Forteana, cryptozoology, paranormal phenomena, and folklore. Founded in 1973 as the Work Group for Ufology, it was converted to a charitable foundation in 1980 and moved to its current location. AFU manages approximately 3.5 kilometers of shelf space containing vast collections of books, magazines, newspaper clippings, photographs, and reports, including Scandinavian and British archives. The organization also collects various related items like scale models, paintings, and memorabilia.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Archives_for_the_Unexplained
